entries.title: typescript-ui
entries.description: TypeScript 6 UI engineering team. Designs, reviews, optimizes, and improves UI code so it looks like a human design team built it — distinctive aesthetic point-of-view, OKLCH color systems, variable typography, motion with spring physics, sub-2.5s LCP, sub-200ms INP, sub-0.1 CLS, and TS6 strict-mode type safety with branded primitives, discriminated unions, and exhaustiveness. Six specialists (design architect, design reviewer, performance engineer, TypeScript engineer, anti-AI auditor, team lead), running on the session model, backed by self-contained references on TS6, Tailwind v4 + OKLCH + APCA, anti-AI-aesthetic patterns, Core Web Vitals, WCAG 2.2 + reduced motion, and component architecture (compound, polymorphic, slot). Four user-invoked skills: typescript-design-ui, typescript-review-ui, typescript-optimize-ui, typescript-improve-ui. Read-only by default; the orchestrator applies findings only on explicit user approval.

entries.title: apple-ui-craft
entries.description: Principal-engineer-grade Apple UI craft for iOS 26. Seven agents, running on the session model (always the strongest available Claude), that design, review, optimize, and craft SwiftUI so it feels like Apple built it -- Liquid Glass, spring animation, intentional haptics, accessibility from birth, SwiftUI performance, and deep platform + cross-platform integration (widgets, Live Activities, App Intents, Apple Intelligence, WebView, watchOS/tvOS/visionOS/CarPlay). Six skills: design-ios (greenfield screen/flow creation), review-ios-ui (HIG + visual + interaction audit), optimize-ios-ui (animation + haptics + performance tuning), craft-ios-ui (full 5-specialist team sweep), audit-accessibility (deep a11y audit), integrate-platform (system-integration audit). Backed by a self-contained reference library across 12 domains (see ARCHITECTURE.md) distilled from an 88-document iOS vault (~103,000 lines), a Context7-verified version-floor registry, GoodMem learnings, and serena navigation. Under ultracode the session model conducts, and Sonnet-5-xhigh executor teams run the scoped grunt stages. Read-only review by default; findings apply only on explicit user approval.

entries.title: typescript-refactor
entries.description: JavaScript-to-TypeScript refactor planning specialist, running on the session model (always the strongest available Claude). Dispatches a full team of 7 read-only senior planner agents in sequential waves (inventory, target architecture, migration slicing, verification) to produce an evidence-tagged phased migration plan, scrum-master-format per-story markdown files, Mermaid dependency DAG, and dispatch wave plan. Grounded in embedded TypeScript reference files covering strict mode, modules, build tooling, testing, monorepos, security, and migration doctrine. Cutting-edge TS practices: strict + noUncheckedIndexedAccess + exactOptionalPropertyTypes, verbatimModuleSyntax, erasable-syntax-only, Zod at boundaries, branded types, Drizzle/Prisma, Fastify/Hono, Vitest, pnpm + turborepo, type-coverage CI gates.

entries.description: Korean-first Claude Code plugin for axhub. onboarding/bootstrap/deploy/import/development/diagnosis/clarity/update 8개 스킬이 ax-hub-cli v0.20.0+ 표면(hidden plugin-support 그룹 + 공개 deploy verify·diagnose·update)을 직접 호출해요. update 는 사전 안내 문장 없이 `현재 버전을 확인할게요.` 로 바로 시작해요. import 는 기존 로컬 앱 가져오기를, diagnosis 는 배포 실패 원인 요약을 담당하고, preview-confirm 배포와 verify 기반 성공 선언을 제공해요.

Cowork hands a task to a local Claude Code agent (and can run approved scripts too); the bridge is idempotent, streams progress, and is token-protected. Install with one command; it sets up a global Cowork skill so every session connects automatically — no /plugin, no setup inside Cowork.

entries.title: v3.4.3
entries.description:
v3.4.3: The Fast and the Secure-ious

FastMCP 3.4.3 closes out a month of SSRF and OAuth hardening: NAT64, 6to4, Teredo, and ISATAP transition addresses can no longer smuggle private IPv4 targets past the SSRF allow-list, Streamable HTTP now validates Host and Origin before session handling to block DNS rebinding against localhost-bound servers, and OAuth redirect validation rejects unsafe schemes and unregistered DCR redirect URIs. Alongside the security work, this release also fixes proxy session teardown races, discriminator-tag handling in JSON schema conversion, and several smaller reliability issues.

Enhancements ✨

  • Dedupe discriminator-required helper across schema converters by @jlowin in #4362
  • Add real Monty sandbox e2e coverage for CodeMode call_tool by @AlexlaGuardia in #4274
  • Switch prettier hook to rbubley/mirrors-prettier by @jlowin in #4366
  • feat(remote): add --verify flag for TLS certificate verification by @jlowin in #4369

Security 🔒

  • fix(deps): clear Dependabot security alerts via lockfile bumps by @jlowin in #4393
  • Clarify resource path parameter safety by @jlowin in #4398
  • Fix dev apps launch escaping by @jlowin in #4399
  • Block NAT64 SSRF bypass by @jlowin in #4400
  • [codex] Fix event store replay isolation by @jlowin in #4402
  • Fix DCR redirect URI validation by @jlowin in #4408
  • Protect streamable HTTP from DNS rebinding by @jlowin in #4405
  • Block unsafe OAuth redirect schemes by @jlowin in #4419
  • Block IPv6 transition SSRF bypasses by @jlowin in #4426

Fixes 🐞

  • fix: caching middleware TypeError on cache miss due to mismatched call_next parameter by @gmenziesint in #4301
  • Fix: async rate limiting middleware get_client_id callbacks by @Chotom in #4319
  • Recognize all GitHub issue-link forms in require-issue-link workflow by @jlowin in #4359
  • fix: preserve required discriminator tags by @he-yufeng in #4297
  • fix(proxy): shield stateful proxy disconnect during session teardown by @jlowin in #4363
  • fix(fs): isolate same-named package imports across providers by @jlowin in #4361
  • fix: StatefulProxyClient.clear() no longer causes KeyError on session teardown by @tcconnally in #4328
  • fix: guard recursive refs in json_schema_to_type by @Epochex in #4312
  • Forward IdP auth errors to MCP client instead of showing HTML error page by @bobbyjames839 in #4293
  • fix(resources): round-trip path values with reserved characters in URI templates by @jlowin in #4368
  • fix: bracket IPv6 hosts in server startup log URL by @jlowin in #4372
  • fix: bound default OIDC discovery timeout and expose it on provider wrappers by @jlowin in #4374
  • fix: validate task tool arguments against declared types by @jlowin in #4373
  • fix(tools): honor serialize_by_alias in tool result serialization by @jlowin in #4391
  • Fix/cimd flow issue by @twjackysu in #4206
  • Reject empty env var keys by @CodingFeng101 in #4410
  • fix: correct replace_type docstring parameter descriptions by @hiSandog in #4375
  • Fix ty 0.0.55 diagnostics and prefab-ui protocol version drift by @jlowin in #4428
  • [codex] Fix OpenAPI resource template requests by @jlowin in #4407

Docs 📚

  • fix: RST docstrings in fastmcp.types render raw on gofastmcp.com by @jlowin in #4367
  • docs: fix 5 broken internal links (auth & providers pages) by @Michael-WhiteCapData in #4344
  • docs: add audit/event-record recipe for tool-call middleware by @AlexlaGuardia in #4345

Dependencies 📦

  • chore(deps): bump actions/checkout from 6 to 7 by @dependabot in #4343
  • chore(deps): bump joserfc from 1.6.5 to 1.6.7 in the uv group across 1 directory by @dependabot in #4394
  • chore(deps): bump joserfc from 1.6.7 to 1.6.8 in the uv group across 1 directory by @dependabot in #4429

Other Changes 🦾

  • Raise fastmcp.ValidationError for invalid tool arguments by @jlowin in #4392
  • Fix versioned auth middleware checks by @jlowin in #4401

New Contributors

  • @gmenziesint made their first contribution in #4301
  • @Chotom made their first contribution in #4319
  • @he-yufeng made their first contribution in #4297
  • @AlexlaGuardia made their first contribution in #4274
  • @tcconnally made their first contribution in #4328
  • @Epochex made their first contribution in #4312
  • @Michael-WhiteCapData made their first contribution in #4344
  • @bobbyjames839 made their first contribution in #4293
  • @twjackysu made their first contribution in #4206
  • @CodingFeng101 made their first contribution in #4410
  • @hiSandog made their first contribution in #4375

Full Changelog: v3.4.2...v3.4.3

entries.title: v3.4.2
entries.description:
v3.4.2: Heads Up

FastMCP 3.4.2 restores JWT compatibility for providers that include private, non-critical JWS header parameters. Tokens from providers like Clerk can carry header metadata such as cat without being rejected before signature and claim validation, while unsupported critical headers are still rejected.

Fixes 🐞

  • Allow private JWT headers by @jlowin in #4290

Docs 📚

  • Docs: add v3.4.1 changelog entries by @jlowin in #4289

Full Changelog: v3.4.1...v3.4.2

entries.title: sherpa
entries.description: Develop like a sherpa: composable /spec, /plan, and /implement skills — one per layer (macro, step, build) — with bundled scout/review/build subagents and adversarial pressure at every boundary. The user composes the workflow; sherpa offers the tools. Nothing runs unasked, nothing persists unless you call /persist. Project-agnostic; extend with project packs.

Compounds knowledge, generates domain agents, monitors its own docs, and surfaces conservative update drift. Orchestrates Claude, Codex, and Oracle through 6 agents, 54 commands, 19 skills, 0 MCP servers. Factory substrate: CXDB turn DAG, scenario bank with satisfaction scoring, evidence pipeline, agent capability policies. Companions: interspect, interphase, interline, interflux, interpath, interwatch, interslack, interform, intercraft, interdev, interpeer, intertest.

Project specifics live in .metate/profile.yml; the skills are codebase-agnostic.

Calibration-free — pair with your own historical project data for tuned estimates.

entries.description: Codebrief intent capture — distills developer intent locally at session end, after a git push, or after a PR is created, and sends only derived annotations (never code) to your Codebrief workspace.

entries.title: leerie
entries.description: Deterministic, headless task orchestrator for Claude Code. Classifies a task, decomposes it into granular subtasks, schedules them into dependency-ordered waves, and executes each in an isolated git worktree under an evidence-gated implement/validate loop. Runs entirely on the Claude Code CLI/subscription via `claude -p` workers.

entries.title: muse-derivative
entries.description: MUSE 衍生写作姊妹包：同人 / 续写 / 番外 / 跨风格改编。入口 derivative-writing 与 muse-writing 主干几乎一致——跑 pipeline 前用 init_derivative_run.py 按 reuse_mode（fan_fiction/sequel/spin_off/cross_style_rewrite）把原作可继承的 phase 设计 yaml 从 canon 蒸馏物 / 用户已有 pipeline 预置到 work_dir，phase1-5 检测到已有 yaml 就在其上完善（否则从零生成），下游消费与 muse-writing 一致。自含整条衍生 pipeline（phase0-7 + 辅助 skill）；可选依赖 muse-canon-distill 取蒸馏物作继承基线。

entries.title: vruum
entries.description: Vruum AI skills + remote MCP server for managed outbound. Slash commands for prospect enrichment and reply diagnosis, paired with the full Vruum MCP tool surface over OAuth 2.1.

entries.title: token-optimizer
entries.description: Audit, fix, and monitor Claude Code context window usage. Find the ghost tokens.

entries.title: agent-orchestra
entries.description: Multi-agent workflow system for Claude Code. Pipeline-based agent orchestration: Issue → Design → Plan → Implement → Review → PR.

entries.title: claude-tdd-pro
entries.description: Drop-in plugin that elevates any codebase to Google's published engineering standards (eng-practices, JS/TS style, Python style). /analyze produces a citable compliance report; /remediate executes a small-CL plan that ratchets the codebase toward the bar; every greenfield prompt is gated by the same RUBRIC.yaml. Hardened against prompt-injection, secret-leakage, and protected-branch commits. Auto-disclosure via Assisted-by: trailer.
